statement.p: Sharell and Henry are working together to paint a room. If Sharell paints the room alone, it would take her 18 hours to complete the job. If Henry paints the room alone, it would take him 9 hours to complete the job. Answer the following question:
If they work together, it would take them hours to complete the job. Use a decimal in your answer if needed.

solution.p: Lets understand how to use fractions to model work problems. Assume it takes a person 5 hours to complete a job. If we treat the whole job as one whole, each hour, this person would complete \frac{1}{5} of the job.
Similarly, if it takes a person x hours to complete a job, each hour, this person would complete \frac{1}{x} of the job.
Now we are ready to model this situation with math.
Its given that, if Sharell paints the room alone, it would take her 18 hours to complete the job. Each hour, Sharell does \frac{1}{18} of the whole job.
Similarly, if Henry paints the room alone, it would take him 9 hours to complete the job. Each hour, Henry does \frac{1}{9} of the whole job.
Assume that, if they work together, it would take them x hours to complete the job. Thus, each hour, they can complete \frac{1}{x} of the whole job.
Now we can write an equation and solve it:
In the second step, to get rid of all fractions, we multiplied each term with the common denominator {18\cdot 9x}.
By the solution we got, if they work together, it would take them {6} hours to complete the job.
